genres.name: Alternative Rock

genres.name: Art Punk

genres.description: Independent guitar rock with a DIY aesthetic.

genres.name: New Wave

genres.name: Shoegaze

genres.name: Progressive Rock
genres.description: Complex, ambitious rock with elaborate compositions and time signatures.

genres.name: Delta Blues

genres.name: Electric Blues
genres.description: Amplified blues guitar music rooted in Chicago and Texas traditions.

genres.name: Texas Blues
genres.description: Blues from Texas with strong shuffle groove and expressive guitar.

genres.name: Jazz Blues

genres.name: Acoustic Blues
genres.description: Unplugged blues using fingerstyle guitar and vocal call-and-response.

genres.name: Baroque
genres.description: Ornate European composition featuring counterpoint, basso continuo, and harpsichord.

genres.description: Large-scale orchestral works with expressive melody and rich harmony.

genres.name: Contemporary Classical
genres.description: Post-1945 composed music ranging from serialism to minimalism.

genres.name: Minimalism
genres.description: Music built from simple repeated patterns that evolve slowly over time.

genres.name: Opera
genres.description: Theatrical vocal performance with full orchestral accompaniment.

genres.name: Chamber Music
genres.description: Composed music for small acoustic ensembles, typically strings.

genres.name: Country
genres.description: American roots music blending folk, blues, and western influences.

genres.name: Bluegrass
genres.description: Acoustic mountain music with high-speed picking and close harmonies.

genres.name: Country Rock

genres.name: Outlaw Country
genres.description: Anti-Nashville country with raw edges and rebellious spirit.

genres.name: Honky Tonk
genres.description: Lively bar-room country with shuffle rhythms and honky-tonk piano.

genres.name: Deep House
genres.description: Warm, soul-influenced house with jazzy chords and deep basslines.

genres.name: Dubstep

genres.description: Futuristic, soul-influenced techno born in Detroit in the 1980s.

genres.name: Psytrance
genres.description: High-energy psychedelic trance with dark, hypnotic basslines.

genres.description: Darker, more aggressive psytrance with sinister sound design.

genres.name: Goa Trance
genres.description: Original psychedelic trance from Goa with spiritual and cosmic themes.

genres.name: Acid Trance
genres.description: Trance built on the squelching sound of the Roland TB-303.

genres.name: Gabber

genres.name: Happy Hardcore
genres.description: Fast, bouncy hardcore with euphoric melodies and chipmunk vocals.

genres.name: Electro House
genres.description: High-energy house music with powerful drops and big-room production.

genres.name: Progressive House
genres.description: Long-form house with extended build-ups and progressive development.

genres.name: French House
genres.description: Disco-influenced house with filtered basslines and funky guitars.

genres.name: Acid House
genres.description: Chicago house built around the Roland TB-303's squelchy acid bass.

genres.description: Stripped-back techno focussing on subtle hypnotic elements and texture.

genres.name: Trap

genres.description: Slow-evolving atmospheric soundscapes focusing on texture and space.

genres.name: Industrial

genres.name: Electro
genres.description: Synth-driven dance music influenced by funk with robotic vocoders.

genres.description: Pop music built on synthesizers and drum machines from the early 80s.

genres.name: EDM

genres.name: Electronica

genres.name: Trap
genres.description: Southern hip-hop sub-genre with dark melodies and triplet hi-hat patterns.

genres.description: Accessible, hook-driven music designed for broad commercial appeal.
